summary: The second edition of Aurélien Géron's 'Hands-On Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn, Keras, and TensorFlow' is a practical guide to building intelligent systems using Python's leading ML libraries. It covers the fundamentals of machine learning in Part I and neural networks/deep learning in Part II, with hands-on examples and exercises throughout.
bullets: Part I introduces ML fundamentals including supervised/unsupervised learning, end-to-end project workflows, and core challenges like overfitting and data quality
Covers classical algorithms in depth: linear/logistic regression, SVMs, decision trees, ensemble methods, random forests, and dimensionality reduction (PCA, LLE)
Explores unsupervised learning techniques including K-Means, DBSCAN, Gaussian mixtures, and anomaly detection
Part II teaches deep learning with Keras and TensorFlow 2, from MLPs and training deep networks to custom models and the tf.data API
Dedicated chapters on CNN architectures (LeNet, AlexNet, GoogLeNet, ResNet, Xception) for computer vision tasks
